context_note: Norway vs Senegal — WC2026 Group D/I. CRITICALLY COMPETITIVE match. Elo diff only 71 points (NOR-SEN). RULE7 does NOT fire. RULE8 FIRES (draw 30.4% >= 25%). Group: Norway 3pts after GD1 win (can absorb draw). Senegal 0pts (MUST get points — plays much more aggressively than Elo suggests).

group_context.simultaneous_match: France vs Iraq (0-0 at time of analysis — France heavy favs at 88%+)
group_context.norway_situation: Can absorb a draw (4pts = very likely qualify). Playing to win but not desperate.
group_context.senegal_situation: MUST get points. A loss essentially eliminates them unless France loses to Iraq (very unlikely). They will attack aggressively from minute 1.
group_context.strategic_implication: Senegal's attacking intensity is ELEVATED beyond Elo model prediction. This is a primary non-Elo input for Q1, Q4, Q5, Q10.

rules_in_effect.RULE7: Does NOT fire — NOR 45.4% and SEN 24.2% are both above 15% threshold
rules_in_effect.RULE8: FIRES — Draw 30.4% >= 25%. Pull all extreme attacking/threshold props 5-8pp toward 50%. Competitive match = tighter, lower-scoring script is more likely than in blowouts.
rules_in_effect.RULE1: Q6 Norway win: RULE1 applies. Market data confirmed at 43-44%. Use market as primary input.
rules_in_effect.GROUP_DYNAMICS: Senegal must-attack context overrides pure Elo model on attacking props. Jackson caught offside 3+ times in GD1 already — Norway's high defensive line will replicate this.
rules_in_effect.RULE15: Not applicable — no blowout, no extreme underdog individual player suppression.

nor_vs_irq.goals_timing: Haaland 29', 43' (1H); Iraq Hussein 39' (1H). Ostigard 76', Hussein OG 90+6' (2H).
nor_vs_irq.tactical_notes: Norway 4-3-3 high press. Dominated possession 61-62%. Scored twice in 1H, managed the 2H with full control. Iraq allowed just 2 shots in the entire 2nd half. Norway's high defensive line = offside trap weapon.

fra_vs_sen.jackson_offsides: 3+ times (multiple confirmed in match reports; one disallowed goal, multiple runs behind French line caught offside)

mane_individual.key_shot: Shot at 40' — one of Senegal's only clear chances
mane_individual.foul_drawn_on_mbappe: Mbappe sought penalty after Mane challenge — not awarded by referee
mane_individual.note: Active but ineffective. Created chance for Sarr near half time (Sarr couldn't convert).

fra_vs_sen.tactical_notes: Senegal compact in 1H (0-0 at HT), then completely broke down in 2H. Conceded 3 goals in last 25 minutes. Koulibaly marshaled the defense; he starts again vs Norway. CRITICAL: Senegal had only 1 total shot in the entire 2nd half vs France.

referee.name: Ismail Elfath (USA, Moroccan-American)
referee.wc_experience: Reffed Portugal vs Ghana at Qatar 2022 — awarded Portugal penalty in 63'
referee.assessment: Above-average card rate. Career penalty rate 0.27/match (1 in ~4 games). This season slightly lower at 0.18/match. NOT a pocket-happy ref but willing to use VAR. KEY: Group D has had ZERO penalties in 2 GD1 matches.

head_to_head.first_competitive_meeting: This match (WC2026) is the first-ever competitive meeting between Norway and Senegal

lineup_notes.norway_predicted_xi: Nyland; Ryerson, Heggem, Ajer, Wolfe; Odegaard, Berge, Aursnes; Sorloth, Haaland, Nusa
lineup_notes.senegal_predicted_xi: Full attacking lineup. Jackson-Mane-Sarr front three. Koulibaly anchors defense.
lineup_notes.haaland_fitness: Played full match vs Iraq, no injury concerns
lineup_notes.mane_fitness: Healthy and fit — fully recovered. This is his redemption WC (missed 2022 entire tournament with injury).
lineup_notes.koulibaly_fitness: Expected to start — KEY matchup vs Haaland.
lineup_notes.odegaard_role: Set piece taker, primary creator. Ryerson takes right-side corners.
lineup_notes.norway_tactical: 4-3-3 high press under Solbakken. High defensive line = offside trap.
lineup_notes.senegal_tactical: 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3. Jackson-Mane-Sarr. Pace and width are weapons. Vulnerable to sustained 2H pressure (showed vs France).

mane_profile.club_form_2025_26: 10+ goals, 6 assists in Saudi Pro League. Won Saudi title.
mane_profile.afcon_2026: Scored vs Egypt in semi-final (Jan 2026). Named Player of Tournament.
mane_profile.foul_drawing_rate: Fouled 3+ times in 6 of 7 recent competitive starts
mane_profile.positional_role: Left winger who cuts inside — NOT a pure box striker. Less likely to score from center box than Jackson.

haaland_profile.koulibaly_matchup: Physical contest vs Senegal captain — will define match tempo

Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.question: Will Senegal be caught offside 2 or more times?
Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.gd1_update: DECISIVE: Jackson alone was caught offside 3+ times in GD1 vs France (confirmed multiple instances including one disallowed goal). Norway's 4-3-3 plays a HIGH DEFENSIVE LINE — identical to the high line that France used to catch Jackson repeatedly. Mane, Sarr, and Jackson are all pace-dependent runners who time runs behind defensive lines = maximum offside exposure.
Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.rule8_adjustment: RULE8 fires but the GD1 evidence is so strong that shrinkage is minimal.
Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.group_dynamics: Senegal MUST attack from minute 1 → forwards commit to runs behind Norway line early and often → more offside exposure than in a neutral match.
Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.counterargument: If Senegal's coach adjusts to play more to feet (Mane receiving, not running behind), offside count drops. But their system relies on pace runs.
Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.crowd_expected_range: 0.45-0.58 (crowd may not appreciate how high Jackson's offside rate was)
Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.edge_direction: STRONG YES — model 53.7% well below GD1+tactical evidence of ~80%+
Q1_sen_2plus_offsides.revision_from_preliminary: 0.52-0.58 → 0.78. Jackson 3+ GD1 + Norway high line is decisive evidence.

Q2_nor_more_2h_goals.question: Will Norway score more goals than Senegal in the second half?
Q2_nor_more_2h_goals.gd1_update: Senegal had 1 total shot in the entire 2H vs France — near-zero 2H attacking output. Norway scored 2 second-half goals vs Iraq (76', 90+6'). In most game scripts where Norway leads at HT (likely given their early scoring), they'll protect the lead while Senegal can't generate 2H chances.
Q2_nor_more_2h_goals.rule8_adjustment: RULE8 fires → pull model 34.4% toward 50% = ~38-42%. But Senegal 2H evidence pushes it further up.
Q2_nor_more_2h_goals.halftime_dependency: If Norway lead at HT: NOR >SEN in 2H is highly likely. If tied or Senegal leads: more uncertain. P(Norway lead at HT) ≈ 40-45% (Norway tends to score early, Haaland 29', 43' in GD1).
Q2_nor_more_2h_goals.counterargument: Haaland scored both GD1 goals in 1H. If Norway protect a lead in 2H and Senegal desperately attacks, it becomes a more even contest. Norway may not score again in 2H if sitting deeper.
Q2_nor_more_2h_goals.edge_direction: YES lean: RULE8 pull + Senegal poor 2H pattern + Norway attacking quality
Q2_nor_more_2h_goals.revision_from_preliminary: 0.38-0.42 → 0.45. Senegal's 1-shot 2H vs France is stronger evidence than RULE8 suppression alone.

Q3_penalty_or_red_card.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown in the match?
Q3_penalty_or_red_card.method: Empirical: Elfath career 0.27 penalty/match. WC2026 avg ~0.40/match. Red card add ~10-15%.
Q3_penalty_or_red_card.gd1_update: BOTH GD1 matches in this group had ZERO penalties and ZERO red cards. Elfath is below his career penalty rate this season (0.18 in 2025/26). Mane challenged Mbappe and no penalty given — this referee did NOT award it even with VAR.
Q3_penalty_or_red_card.referee_context: Elfath: 0.27 penalties/match career but only 0.18 this season. Not pocket-happy. WC2022: did award a penalty vs Portugal-Ghana (so he CAN give them). Fouls/match ~22 = busy but not card-happy at WC rate.
Q3_penalty_or_red_card.elevating_factors: Must-win Senegal = desperation late → cynical fouls possible. Haaland draws 3+ fouls/match — some in the box. Mane drawn contact before (Mbappe incident).
Q3_penalty_or_red_card.suppressing_factors: Group D's zero penalty trend in GD1. Elfath below career rate this season. Two clean GD1 matches suggest refereeing culture in this group is lenient.
Q3_penalty_or_red_card.edge_direction: SLIGHT NO lean: group zero-penalty trend + Elfath's below-career-rate season
Q3_penalty_or_red_card.revision_from_preliminary: 0.40-0.46 → 0.38. Group zero-penalty GD1 data and Elfath's current season rate pull lower.

Q4_sen_more_fouls_than_nor.question: Will Senegal commit more fouls than Norway?
Q4_sen_more_fouls_than_nor.gd1_update: SIGNIFICANT TENSION: Senegal committed only 5 fouls vs France (vs France's 8). Norway committed 13 vs Iraq. But opponent quality matters enormously — France were a harder pressing team than Norway, and Iraq was more physical than Senegal will be. Direct comparison is unreliable.
Q4_sen_more_fouls_than_nor.key_context: Koulibaly defending Haaland physically → Koulibaly fouls Haaland in the box. Norway's high press means THEY foul in midfield too. Mane fouls in 26 of 33 tournament appearances. Elfath calls ~22 fouls/match combined (11 each at average). Ryerson (Norway RB) commits 2+ fouls in 6 of last 7 Norway starts.
Q4_sen_more_fouls_than_nor.must_attack_effect: Senegal must attack → they have the ball at times → they press when defending → pressing = fouling. But Norway with Haaland creates more foul opportunities FOR Senegal when they defend.
Q4_sen_more_fouls_than_nor.edge_direction: Coin flip. Lean slightly NO (Norway fouls more based on their pressing style and GD1 foul count).
Q4_sen_more_fouls_than_nor.revision_from_preliminary: 0.48-0.52 → 0.48. GD1 shows Senegal as a low-fouling team (5 vs France). Norway more foul-prone (13 vs Iraq). Even accounting for opponent differences, slight lean to Norway fouling more.

Q5_4plus_total_sot_2h.question: Will there be 4 or more total shots on target in the second half?
Q5_4plus_total_sot_2h.gd1_update: Senegal had 1 total shot in 2H vs France. Iraq had ~2 total shots in 2H vs Norway. But these were blowout match scripts where the losing team shut down. NOR vs SEN is a COMPETITIVE match where Senegal must attack in 2H → much higher expected Senegal 2H SOT than vs France. Norway's attacking quality (Haaland) also generates 2H shots.
Q5_4plus_total_sot_2h.rule8_adjustment: RULE8 fires but it pulls both ways — draw-heavy = competitive = both teams creating chances in 2H.
Q5_4plus_total_sot_2h.senegal_must_attack: Senegal MUST score → they'll generate 2H shots desperately. Even vs France they had only 1 2H shot but they were already 0-3 down and demoralized. Vs Norway in a must-win, they'll push harder even if trailing.
Q5_4plus_total_sot_2h.edge_direction: Slight YES: competitive open game + Senegal must-attack context in 2H
Q5_4plus_total_sot_2h.revision_from_preliminary: 0.48-0.52 → 0.55. Competitive match dynamics + Senegal must-attack in 2H elevates above model. Norway's Haaland adds 2H SOT regardless.

Q6_nor_win.question: Will Norway win the match?
Q6_nor_win.method: RULE1: avg(crowd, sharp market). Market: 43-44%. Model: 45.4%.

market_data.btts_implied: 54% — suggests competitive game, not a blowout

Q6_nor_win.gd1_update: Norway dominant (4-1 vs Iraq). Senegal lost (1-3 to France). But Iraq and France are very different opponents. Norway's performance vs Iraq overstates their edge vs Senegal.
Q6_nor_win.haaland_factor: Haaland vs Koulibaly is the defining physical contest. If Haaland wins = Norway likely wins.
Q6_nor_win.edge_direction: Aligned with market and model. No strong edge — submit model/market consensus.
Q6_nor_win.revision_from_preliminary: 0.48-0.52 → 0.45. Market is at 43-44%; Polymarket $3.6M confirms this. Our model also 45.4%. Converge on 0.45.

Q7_ht_tied.question: At halftime, will the match be tied?
Q7_ht_tied.method: Double Poisson: P(any tied HT score)=46.2%.
Q7_ht_tied.gd1_update: Norway scored in 29' AND 43' vs Iraq (both 1H). They were leading 2-1 at HT. Haaland is an early-game threat. France vs Senegal WAS 0-0 at HT (all goals after 66') — supporting the HT tie scenario for Senegal matches.
Q7_ht_tied.competing_evidence: Norway's GD1 shows they CAN score in the first 45 mins (2 goals). BUT vs Iraq (much weaker). Senegal's defense with Koulibaly will be harder to break early. 0-0 or 1-1 at HT is plausible. Norway-first-half dominance is the primary risk for NO.
Q7_ht_tied.wc_empirical: WC group stage HT tie rate historically ~40-45%. Model 46.2% is at the higher end.
Q7_ht_tied.edge_direction: Slight NO lean: Norway's early-goal tendency (scored 29', 43' in GD1) makes a NOR lead at HT plausible.
Q7_ht_tied.revision_from_preliminary: 0.42-0.46 → 0.42. Norway's GD1 showed them scoring twice in first 45 — suggests they lead at HT more often than draw. Haaland is an early-game threat.

Q8_9plus_corners.question: Will there be 9 or more total corner kicks?
Q8_9plus_corners.method: Revised Poisson multiplier 3.3: lambda_total=7.38, P(>=9)=34.4%. Model with 2.6 was too low (13.5%).
Q8_9plus_corners.gd1_update: France vs Senegal full match: France 6 corners, Senegal 4 corners = 10 TOTAL. Already above 9. Norway vs Iraq: Norway corner count not confirmed but Odegaard's corner led to 76' goal; Norway averaged 5.6/match. Iraq ~2-3 corners in full match.
Q8_9plus_corners.combined_averages: Norway averages 5.6 corners/match. Senegal averages 6.1 corners taken/match. Combined projection = ~10-11 expected. P(9+) at this expectation is ~55-60%.
Q8_9plus_corners.must_attack_effect: Senegal attacking desperately = more crosses into box = more corners. Norway's wide attackers (Nusa on left, Odegaard) also generate corners.
Q8_9plus_corners.market_signal: Corners market line at ~9-10 total (Robinhood/Kalshi). Right at the threshold.
Q8_9plus_corners.counterargument: Model underestimates expected corners vs team averages. But GD1 France-Senegal had 10 total supporting the higher number. However France-Senegal had weaker teams defensively clearing corners.
Q8_9plus_corners.edge_direction: Near coin-flip. Combined averages favor YES; model alone says NO. Market right at the line.
Q8_9plus_corners.revision_from_preliminary: 0.28-0.35 → 0.50. Fra-Sen GD1 had 10 total corners (above 9 already). Combined team averages project 10-11. Market at the borderline. This is genuinely ~50/50.

Q9_nor_6plus_sot.question: Will Norway have 6 or more shots on target?
Q9_nor_6plus_sot.gd1_update: Norway had 5 SOT vs Iraq in GD1 (from 12 shots). Haaland alone had 4 SOT. They fell 1 short of 6 against a MUCH weaker opponent (Iraq: 1-4 loss, only 1 SOT). Senegal's defense is meaningfully better than Iraq's — Koulibaly, organized shape.
Q9_nor_6plus_sot.haaland_factor: Haaland generated 4 SOT vs Iraq. He will generate 2-3 SOT vs Senegal even if Koulibaly is effective. But 3 additional SOT from teammates needed for total 6.
Q9_nor_6plus_sot.open_game_effect: Senegal must attack = they leave space on counter → Norway get more shots on transition. Could push NOR toward 5-6 SOT. But Senegal's defensive organization is better than Iraq's.
Q9_nor_6plus_sot.counterargument: 5 SOT vs Iraq (the weakest team) = ceiling for Norway given they typically score from limited chances (clinical). Model gives 18.2% for 6+. The open-game dynamic could push toward 30-38%.
Q9_nor_6plus_sot.edge_direction: SLIGHT NO: Norway baseline is 5 SOT vs weak Iraq. 6+ vs stronger Senegal requires exceeding GD1 output.
Q9_nor_6plus_sot.revision_from_preliminary: 0.30-0.40 → 0.38. Norway had 5 SOT in GD1 vs weaker opponent. Open game dynamic from Senegal must-attack partially offsets Senegal's better defense. Settle just below 40%.

Q10_mane_scores.question: Will Sadio Mane score a goal (excluding own goals)?
Q10_mane_scores.method: Poisson: lambda_Mane (35% of SEN lambda)=0.342, P>=1=28.9%.
Q10_mane_scores.gd1_update: Mane did NOT score vs France in GD1. Had 1 shot on target (40' effort). Created chance for Sarr near HT. Active but ineffective. Norway conceded 0 goals to Senegal-caliber attacks in their defensive shape.
Q10_mane_scores.wc_career_record: 1 goal in 4 career WC matches = 0.25 goals/WC game. Scored only vs Japan in 2018.
Q10_mane_scores.mane_profile: 34 years old, left winger (cuts inside), NOT a pure box striker. Jackson is the primary penalty-area target. Mane draws fouls (3+ in 6 of 7 recent starts) but creates more than he finishes in big matches.
Q10_mane_scores.must_win_boost: This is Mane's last WC chance (missed 2022 entirely with injury). Maximum motivation. Will take shots, will be in dangerous positions. If Senegal get a penalty → Mane likely taker → big probability boost.
Q10_mane_scores.norway_defense: Koulibaly vs Haaland is the key matchup, but Norway's CBs and Ryerson will track Mane. Norway conceded just 1 goal in GD1 to an inferior Iraq attack.
Q10_mane_scores.counterargument: Mane is the player most likely to create something out of nothing (55 career international goals). His pace and dribbling can beat Ryerson. At +310, the market may slightly undervalue his in a must-win scenario.
Q10_mane_scores.edge_direction: ALIGN WITH MARKET: market at ~24% is well-informed. Model 28.9% slightly above market. Settle near market.
Q10_mane_scores.revision_from_preliminary: 0.26-0.32 → 0.24. Market +310 is the sharpest signal. GD1 no goal. WC record 1/4. Settle at market implied probability.

Q1.notes: STRONGEST EDGE IN BATCH. Jackson caught offside 3+ times in GD1 alone. Norway plays the exact same high defensive line that France used. Senegal must-attack from minute 1. Near-certainty.

Q2.notes: Model 34.4% + RULE8 pull + Senegal's 1-shot 2H vs France. Norway scored 2 in 2H vs Iraq. YES is the right direction.

Q3.notes: Elfath 0.27/match career but only 0.18 this season. Zero penalties in 2 GD1 matches in this group. Slight NO lean. Must-win desperation elevates but doesn't overcome trend.

Q4.notes: Genuine coin flip. SEN only 5 fouls in GD1 (very low). NOR 13 fouls in GD1. But matchup context differs. Submit near 50%.

Q5.notes: Competitive open game. Senegal must-attack in 2H. Norway's attacking quality. RULE8 slightly shrinks but competitive dynamic offsets. Slight YES.

Q6.notes: Market 43-44%, Polymarket $3.6M at 43%, model 45.4%. No strong edge. Submit 0.45 — Norway is the most likely single outcome but still below 50%.

Q7.notes: Norway scored in 29' and 43' in GD1 — early scorers. A Norway HT lead is the single most likely HT state. Model 46.2% is slightly high. Revise to 0.42.

Q8.notes: FRA-SEN had 10 total corners. Combined team averages project 10-11. Market at the 9-10 line. Genuine 50/50. Submit at 0.50.

Q9.notes: Norway only got 5 SOT vs much weaker Iraq. 6+ vs Senegal (better defense, Koulibaly) requires exceeding GD1 output. Open-game dynamic partially offsets. Submit 0.38.

Q10.notes: Market +310 (~24%). 1 WC goal in 4 career matches. GD1: no goal from Mane. Must-win context is positive but market has priced it in. Align with market at 0.24.

edge_ranking.1_Q1_offside: 0.78 YES — highest confidence. Jackson 3+ GD1 offsides + Norway high line = near certainty
edge_ranking.2_Q6_nor_win: 0.45 — aligned with sharp market ($3.6M Polymarket). No model edge but accurate.
edge_ranking.3_Q9_nor_sot: 0.38 NO lean — Norway only got 5 SOT vs weaker Iraq; 6+ vs stronger Senegal unlikely
edge_ranking.4_Q2_nor_2h_goals: 0.45 YES — Senegal's 1-shot 2H vs France. NOR scored 2 in 2H vs Iraq.
edge_ranking.5_Q10_mane: 0.24 NO lean — market +310, 1 WC goal in 4 career, no GD1 goal
edge_ranking.6_Q5_2h_sot: 0.55 YES — open competitive game; Senegal must-attack 2H
edge_ranking.7_Q3_penalty_rc: 0.38 NO — zero penalties in group GD1, Elfath below-career-rate season
edge_ranking.8_Q7_ht_tie: 0.42 slight NO — Norway scored 29' and 43' in GD1 (early scorers)
edge_ranking.9_Q8_corners: 0.50 coin flip — combined averages project 10-11; market at the 9-10 line
edge_ranking.10_Q4_sen_fouls: 0.48 coin flip — genuine uncertainty; align near 50%

post_match_results.match_facts: Norway won 3-2. Full scoreline confirmed. Final score shows BTTS YES (both teams scored) — a real match, not a blowout. Senegal scored 2 goals in this match (more than the 1-shot 2H vs France suggested possible). Corner count confirmed at 9+ (Q8 YES). Norway confirmed 6+ SOT (Q9 YES).

Q1.question: Will Senegal be caught offside 2+ times?
Q1.notes: BIGGEST WIN OF THE MATCH. Us 78% vs crowd 50% = +22.07 RBP. Jackson's GD1 offsides and Norway's high line called perfectly. This was the best single-question call across all sessions today.

Q2.question: Will Norway score more 2H goals than Senegal?
Q2.notes: Outcome NO — both teams scored in some half distribution. Norway did NOT outscore Senegal in 2H specifically. Our 45% estimate was right direction but tied with crowd. Small RBP gain.

Q3.question: Penalty kick OR red card?
Q3.notes: No penalty or red card in the match. Our 38% NO lean was correct. Small edge vs crowd (39%). Group D penalty drought confirmed.

Q4.question: Will Senegal commit more fouls than Norway?
Q4.notes: Senegal committed FEWER fouls than Norway. Our 48% (vs crowd 55%) was the right lean — we correctly suspected Norway fouled more (13 fouls in GD1). +8.46 RBP. Crowd over-estimated Senegal's fouls.

Q5.question: 4+ total 2H SOT?
Q5.notes: YES — 4+ total 2H SOT happened. Our 55% was correct direction but crowd at 64% was more confident in the right outcome. Small loss. This was a competitive Norway 3-2 scoreline suggesting high 2H activity.

Q6.question: Norway win?
Q6.notes: Norway won. Both we (0.45) and crowd (0.49) were below 50%, so both were surprised by the win. Tiny loss vs crowd. Norway winning at 3-2 was an exciting match result.

Q7.question: HT tied?
Q7.notes: HT was NOT tied — Norway likely led at HT (given final 3-2 and their early goal tendency: 29', 43' in GD1). Our 42% NO lean was correct. Small gain vs crowd.

Q8.question: 9+ total corners?
Q8.notes: 9+ corners occurred. Tiny loss (crowd was marginally more confident). Our 50% coin-flip estimate was essentially correct — we correctly revised up from initial 13.5% model output. Negligible vs crowd.

Q9.question: Norway 6+ SOT?
Q9.notes: Norway had 6+ SOT in the match. We underestimated (0.38) — Norway's open-game SOT opportunity in a 3-2 match exceeded our GD1-calibrated estimate (5 SOT vs weaker Iraq). Norway generating 6+ SOT in a competitive 3-2 fight vs a good Senegal team is expected. Small loss.

Q10.question: Sadio Mané scores?
Q10.notes: Mané did NOT score despite Senegal scoring 2 goals (own goals or other Senegal scorers). Our WC-record-based 0.24 estimate vs crowd 0.30 = +5.63 RBP. The WC goal-record distinction (1 in 4 WC matches vs 0.43/cap career) paid off.

analysis.biggest_win: Q1 +22.07 — Jackson offside data from GD1 + Norway high line. Single best call of the entire 2026-06-22 session.
analysis.biggest_loss: Q5 -5.30 — we said 55%, crowd 64%, YES occurred. Both correct direction but crowd was more confident.
what_worked: Q1: GD1 offside behavioral data is a near-perfect predictor when the tactical context replicates (high line vs pace runners). 0.78 vs crowd 0.50 = +22.07.
Q4: Norway fouls more than Senegal — recognized from Norway GD1 (13 fouls) vs Senegal GD1 (5 fouls). +8.46.
Q10: WC-specific vs career record distinction for Mané. 1 WC goal in 4 matches. +5.63.
Q7: Norway scores early (GD1: 29', 43') → HT not tied. +3.29.
Q3: Group D zero-penalty trend continued. Elfath below career rate. +2.91.
what_missed: Q5: Should have pushed harder on 2H SOT YES. Senegal had to score 2 goals = they generated shots. Our 55% was correct direction but crowd at 64% was better calibrated.
Q9: Norway 6+ SOT in a 3-2 competitive match is expected. Our GD1 ceiling estimate (5 SOT vs Iraq) was too conservative. Should have revised further toward 45-50% given the open-game dynamics.
Q6: Both we and crowd were below 50% on Norway winning. No one called the outcome well — small loss.
Q8: Corners at 50% was right; crowd at 53% was marginally better. No real miss.

rule_validations.Q1_offside_rule: VALIDATED — GD1 behavioral data for 'offside-prone' forwards vs high lines is near-deterministic. Consider making this an explicit rule: if forward caught offside 2+ times in GD1 AND opponent plays high line, submit 0.75-0.85 YES on 2+ offsides.
rule_validations.Q4_foul_rate_data: VALIDATED — foul rate data (footystats recent 10) correctly identified Norway as the higher-fouling team. +8.46 reward. Always pull foul/game data for 'who fouls more' questions.
rule_validations.Q10_wc_record: VALIDATED — separating WC-specific record from career average on individual scoring questions is meaningful. Mané 0.43 career vs 0.25 WC should always be the 'true probability' for WC predictions.
